Discord Notifications on Mac: Turn Them On and Tame the Server Noise

Lately a lot of services and my own company have migrated to Discord, so here's a quick note on how to enable Discord notifications on macOS and tame channel-level noise once things start flooding in.
Discord Quick Start: A Hands-on Walkthrough of the Community App

I've been moving over to Discord lately — I used to live on Slack — so I figured I'd write a basic Discord usage walkthrough covering the home view, servers, channels, threads, and a few other essentials.
How to Find Your Discord ID (Desktop + Mobile)

I was building a Discord Bot recently and noticed that finding your own Discord User ID is weirdly fiddly — and a lot of people get the wrong thing. So this post walks through how to grab it correctly, including the mobile flow.
Why Writing Posts Can Earn Money — Google AdSense and Liker, Explained

Did you know writing blog posts can earn you money? And did you know Google AdSense isn't the only path? This post is the intro version of how it works — Google AdSense (click revenue vs. impression revenue), how AdBlock affects you, what it takes to build real income, plus Liker as a like-based crypto-tipping alternative.
How to Change Chrome DevTools' Language

Chrome DevTools has always defaulted to English, but starting around Chrome 94 it added language localization. This post is the quick how-to — opening DevTools settings, switching to your preferred language under Preferences, and reloading DevTools to apply.
HMAC Authentication in Postman With Pre-Request Scripts (TDX API Example)

HMAC authentication is common for public APIs that need request-level signing, but Postman doesn't ship with HMAC support out of the box. This post walks through implementing HMAC in Postman via a pre-request script — using Taiwan's TDX (transport data) API as the concrete example, with jsSHA loaded via CDN inside the script.
Stop Letting Them Quietly Collect My Data — Disabling Personalized Ads on Google and Facebook

Heads up — this isn't a "block all ads" post. It's a "limit how much these platforms quietly collect about you" post. Covers Google's ad personalization controls (the activity controls + ad settings page), my actual recommendation (counterintuitively — keep personalization on but tune it), and Facebook's privacy checkup for hiding specific advertisers and topics.
Quickly Clear Old Form Data or Stale Email History from Your Browser

Browsers love to suggest every email and username you (or anyone using your computer) ever typed. This post covers the Shift+Delete trick to remove individual autocomplete entries in Firefox and Chrome without nuking your whole browsing history.
Using Firefox for Frontend Dev: Features and Advantages

People keep asking what browser I use and why it isn't Chrome. So here's a quick post on Firefox's frontend dev features — better-than-Chrome Flex tooling, CSS Grid inspection, event listener inspection per element, accessibility tools like color blindness simulation, in-browser font tools, and an AJAX inspector that lets you edit and resend requests directly.
Discord Role Bot Tutorial: Auto-Assign Roles via Invite Links (Invite-Role Bot)

When you want new members to automatically get a specific role based on which invite link they used, vanilla Discord can't do it on its own — you need a bot. This post walks through using Invite-Role Bot to wire invite links to roles.
